KXCPI-26MAY-T0.4.reason: Monthly CPI above 0.4% remains common with tariff pass-through and sticky shelter costs, but slight downside risk from cooling demand and energy price declines.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.5.reason: Tariff-driven goods inflation lingers into May 2026, nudging the >0.5% probability marginally above Kalshi consensus.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.6.reason: Upside tail risk remains from a potential energy spike or renewed tariff escalation, so fair value sits slightly above market.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.7.reason: A 0.7%+ monthly print would require simultaneous energy, food, and shelter surges; unlikely but slightly underpriced at 2c given ongoing tariff uncertainty.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.8.reason: Extreme monthly CPI surge of 0.8%+ is very improbable barring a severe supply shock; fair value matches the 2c Kalshi price.
